title: SAC Armed Forces Day: Soe Win Announces Imminent Presidential Selection
detail: On Myanmar Armed Forces Day (March 27), Vice-Senior General Soe Win announced that a president and vice presidents would be selected within days and that state responsibilities would transfer to a nominal civilian government. Min Aung Hlaing is widely expected to assume the presidency. The announcement followed the convening of a junta-packed parliament on March 16, seated after elections that the NUG, ASEAN, the US, EU, and UN condemned as illegitimate and non-representative. Analysts assessed the transfer as a cosmetic rebranding rather than a genuine power shift — the military retains 25% of parliamentary seats under the 2008 constitution, and Min Aung Hlaing would maintain ultimate authority as president. The NUG rejected the transition as theater designed to secure ASEAN re-engagement and international legitimacy while the military continues its armed campaign.

title: AA Advances Deepen Sittwe Siege — Fighters Within 2 km of City Center
detail: Reporting from March 27 confirmed that Arakan Army forces continued pressing their assault on Sittwe and surrounding areas, with fighters now positioned within approximately 2 km of the city center. Clashes were concentrated around Shwe Min Gan Port and Sat Yoe Kya Creek. The junta's Western Regional Military Command, already displaced from Ann Township (captured December 2024), remains trapped in Sittwe as the AA tightens its cordon. The Kyaukpyu area — location of China's strategic CMEC deep-sea port megaproject — was also reported as contested. Al Jazeera published a comprehensive analysis of the multi-front war on March 27, noting internal Kokang vs. Ta'ang clashes in mid-March followed by a ceasefire, highlighting growing internal resistance tensions alongside the Sittwe campaign.
